title: Cyathocalyx philippinensis
taxonomy: Cyathocalyx philippinensis is classified within the family Annonaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. The genus Cyathocalyx is a part of the Annonaceae family and is native to Southeast Asia. Cyathocalyx philippinensis is a species within this genus and is native to the Philippines. The classification of Cyathocalyx philippinensis highlights its unique position within the Annonaceae family.

intro: Cyathocalyx philippinensis, also known as the Philippine Cyathocalyx, is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae that is commonly found in the tropical forests of the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyathocalyx philippinensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been isolated and studied for their potential medicinal applications. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory infections and digestive issues.
